title: Typed error variants and downcast for error classification
description:
Replace substring-scanning error classification with typed `thiserror` variants and downcasting (report highest-leverage fix #4 — the single largest stringly cluster, 5 sites).

## Sites
- `graph.rs:26-33` — `GraphError::CycleDetected`/`NodeNotFound` already exist and reach `main.rs` downcastable; reuse them.
- `storage/memory.rs` — add `IssueNotFoundError`/`GateNotFoundError` typed errors at the `anyhow!` origins (and the matching `JsonFileStorage` paths).
- `main.rs:74-104` (`error_to_exit_code`), `main.rs:173`, `main.rs:1298`, `main.rs:1737-1742`, `main.rs:2231-2236` — replace `error_msg.contains(...)` dispatch with `downcast_ref` against the typed variants.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Issue-not-found, gate-not-found, and cycle-detected conditions are represented by typed error variants, surfaced via `anyhow` downcasting.
- [ ] All five `error_msg.contains(...)` / substring-match classification sites in `main.rs` are replaced by `downcast_ref` checks; no control-flow decision is driven by matching against a human-readable error string.
- [ ] Exit-code mapping (`error_to_exit_code`) is driven by typed errors and preserves the existing exit codes for each condition.
- [ ] `cargo-ci` and `code-review` pass; error-message text shown to users is unchanged where it was already correct.
